speaker: SICINIUS
text: We hear not of him, neither need we fear him.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: His remedies are tame—the present peace,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And quietness of the people, which before

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Were in wild hurry. Here do we make his friends

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Blush that the world goes well, who rather had,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Though they themselves did suffer by ’t, behold

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Dissentious numbers pest’ring streets than see

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Our tradesmen singing in their shops and going

speaker: SICINIUS
text: About their functions friendly.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We stood to ’t in good time.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is this Menenius?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: ’Tis he, ’tis he. O, he is grown most kind

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Of late.—Hail, sir.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Hail to you both.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Your Coriolanus is not much missed

speaker: SICINIUS
text: But with his friends. The commonwealth doth stand,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And so would do were he more angry at it.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: All’s well, and might have been much better if

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He could have temporized.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Where is he, hear you?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Nay, I hear nothing;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: His mother and his wife hear nothing from him.

speaker:
ALL
               
              CITIZENS
text: The gods preserve you both!

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Good e’en, our neighbors.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good e’en to you all, good e’en to you all.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Ourselves, our wives, and children, on our knees

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: Are bound to pray for you both.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Live, and thrive!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Farewell, kind neighbors. We wished Coriolanus

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Had loved you as we did.

speaker:
ALL
               
              CITIZENS
text: Now the gods keep you!

speaker:
BOTH
               
              TRIBUNES
text: Farewell, farewell.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: This is a happier and more comely time

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Than when these fellows ran about the streets

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Crying confusion.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Caius Martius was

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A worthy officer i’ th’ war, but insolent,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O’ercome with pride, ambitious, past all thinking

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Self-loving.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And affecting one sole throne, without assistance.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I think not so.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We should by this, to all our lamentation,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: If he had gone forth consul, found it so.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The gods have well prevented it, and Rome

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sits safe and still without him.

speaker: AEDILE
text: Worthy tribunes,

speaker: AEDILE
text: There is a slave, whom we have put in prison,

speaker: AEDILE
text: Reports the Volsces with two several powers

speaker: AEDILE
text: Are entered in the Roman territories,

speaker: AEDILE
text: And with the deepest malice of the war

speaker: AEDILE
text: Destroy what lies before ’em.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: ’Tis Aufidius,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Who, hearing of our Martius’ banishment,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Thrusts forth his horns again into the world,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which were inshelled when Martius stood for Rome,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And durst not once peep out.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Come, what talk you of Martius?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go see this rumorer whipped. It cannot be

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The Volsces dare break with us.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Cannot be?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: We have record that very well it can,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And three examples of the like hath been

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Within my age. But reason with the fellow

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Before you punish him, where he heard this,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Lest you shall chance to whip your information

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And beat the messenger who bids beware

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of what is to be dreaded.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Tell not me.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: I know this cannot be.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Not possible.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The nobles in great earnestness are going

speaker: MESSENGER
text: All to the Senate House. Some news is coming

speaker: MESSENGER
text: That turns their countenances.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: ’Tis this slave—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Go whip him ’fore the people’s eyes—his raising,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Nothing but his report.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Yes, worthy sir,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The slave’s report is seconded, and more,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: More fearful, is delivered.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: What more fearful?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: It is spoke freely out of many mouths—

speaker: MESSENGER
text: How probable I do not know—that Martius,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Joined with Aufidius, leads a power ’gainst Rome

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And vows revenge as spacious as between

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The young’st and oldest thing.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: This is most likely!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Raised only that the weaker sort may wish

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good Martius home again.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: The very trick on ’t.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This is unlikely;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He and Aufidius can no more atone

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Than violent’st contrariety.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: You are sent for to the Senate.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: A fearful army, led by Caius Martius

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Associated with Aufidius, rages

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Upon our territories, and have already

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: O’erborne their way, consumed with fire and took

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: What lay before them.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: O, you have made good work!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What news? What news?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You have holp to ravish your own daughters and

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To melt the city leads upon your pates,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To see your wives dishonored to your noses—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What’s the news? What’s the news?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Your temples burnèd in their cement, and

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Your franchises, whereon you stood, confined

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Into an auger’s bore.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Pray now, your news?—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You have made fair work, I fear me.—Pray, your news?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If Martius should be joined with Volscians—

speaker: COMINIUS
text: If?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He is their god; he leads them like a thing

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Made by some other deity than Nature,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That shapes man better; and they follow him

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Against us brats with no less confidence

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Than boys pursuing summer butterflies

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Or butchers killing flies.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You have made good work,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You and your apron-men, you that stood so much

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Upon the voice of occupation and

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The breath of garlic eaters!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He’ll shake your Rome about your ears.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As Hercules did shake down mellow fruit.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You have made fair work.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But is this true, sir?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Ay, and you’ll look pale

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Before you find it other. All the regions

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Do smilingly revolt, and who resists

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Are mocked for valiant ignorance

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And perish constant fools. Who is ’t can blame him?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Your enemies and his find something in him.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: We are all undone, unless

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The noble man have mercy.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Who shall ask it?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The Tribunes cannot do ’t for shame; the people

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Deserve such pity of him as the wolf

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Does of the shepherds. For his best friends, if they

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Should say “Be good to Rome,” they charged him even

speaker: COMINIUS
text: As those should do that had deserved his hate

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And therein showed like enemies.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: ’Tis true.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If he were putting to my house the brand

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That should consume it, I have not the face

speaker: MENENIUS
text: To say “Beseech you, cease.”—You have made fair hands,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You and your crafts! You have crafted fair!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You have brought

speaker: COMINIUS
text: A trembling upon Rome such as was never

speaker: COMINIUS
text: S’ incapable of help.

speaker: TRIBUNES
text: Say not we brought it.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: How? Was ’t we? We loved him, but like beasts

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And cowardly nobles, gave way unto your clusters,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Who did hoot him out o’ th’ city.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: But I fear

speaker: COMINIUS
text: They’ll roar him in again. Tullus Aufidius,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The second name of men, obeys his points

speaker: COMINIUS
text: As if he were his officer. Desperation

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Is all the policy, strength, and defense

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That Rome can make against them.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Here come the clusters.—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And is Aufidius with him? You are they

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That made the air unwholesome when you cast

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Your stinking, greasy caps in hooting at

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Coriolanus’ exile. Now he’s coming,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And not a hair upon a soldier’s head

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which will not prove a whip. As many coxcombs

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As you threw caps up will he tumble down

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And pay you for your voices. ’Tis no matter.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If he could burn us all into one coal,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: We have deserved it.

speaker:
ALL
               
              CITIZENS
text: Faith, we hear fearful news.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: For mine own part,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: When I said banish him, I said ’twas pity.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: And so did I.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: And so did I. And, to say the truth, so

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: did very many of us. That we did we did for the

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: best; and though we willingly consented to his

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CITIZEN
text: banishment, yet it was against our will.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You’re goodly things, you voices!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You have made good work, you and your cry!—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Shall ’s to the Capitol?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: O, ay, what else?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Go, masters, get you home. Be not dismayed.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: These are a side that would be glad to have

speaker: SICINIUS
text: This true which they so seem to fear. Go home,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And show no sign of fear.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: The gods be good to us! Come, masters,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: let’s home. I ever said we were i’ th’ wrong when

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CITIZEN
text: we banished him.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CITIZEN
text: So did we all. But, come, let’s home.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I do not like this news.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Nor I.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let’s to the Capitol. Would half my wealth

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Would buy this for a lie.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Pray, let’s go.
